responses: 👋 Hello, Gentinian! I am Tibby, GTDLNHS' cutest chatbot. How may I help you today?

🏫 GTDLNHS caters to students from Grade 7 to Grade 12.
📚 We follow the new curriculum aligned with DepEd guidelines.
🎓 Instead of traditional strands, we offer the Strengthened Senior High School Program with career pathways like:
• Bakery Operations
• Business and Entrepreneurship
• Creative and Multimedia Arts
• Culinary Arts
• Engineering
• Food and Beverage Services
• Health Sciences
• Hospitality and Tourism
• Social Science and Humanities
• Software Development

🎓 We serve Grade 7-12 students with the Strengthened Senior High School Program! This prepares you for exciting career paths including Culinary Arts, Engineering, Health Sciences, Software Development, and more. We follow DepEd's latest curriculum guidelines.

🏫 GTDLNHS offers education from Grade 7 to Grade 12. Our Senior High program features 10 career pathways like Business, Creative Arts, Engineering, Health Sciences, and Hospitality—designed to prepare you for your future!
🏫 Nag-aalok kami ng Grade 7 hanggang Grade 12. May Strengthened Senior High School Program kami with career pathways like Culinary Arts, Engineering, Health Sciences, at Software Development!
📍 General Tiburcio de Leon National High School is located at Corner Mercado St., General T. de Leon, Valenzuela City, Metro Manila, Philippines.
📍 You can find us at Corner Mercado St., General T. de Leon, Valenzuela City, Metro Manila. See you on campus!
📍 We're located in Valenzuela City at Corner Mercado St., General T. de Leon, Metro Manila, Philippines. Easy to find!
📍 Nandito kami sa Corner Mercado St., General T. de Leon, Valenzuela City, Metro Manila. Kita tayo sa campus!

patterns: what do i need to graduate
what do i need to move up
ano kailangan para mag move up
responses: 🐾 To move up, you must complete the required academic level and submit important documents like your Certificate of Completion and Certificate of Good Moral Character. To graduate from Senior High, you need to complete all required subjects for both Grade 11 and 12, and meet academic and behavioral standards.

📋 Moving up requires completing your grade level plus submitting your Certificate of Completion and Good Moral Character. For SHS graduation, you must finish all Grade 11 and 12 subjects while maintaining good academic and behavioral standing.

🎓 Graduation requirements include completing all Grade 11 and 12 subjects, maintaining good academic performance, and meeting behavioral standards. For moving up, submit your Certificate of Completion and Good Moral Character on time!

🐾 Para mag-move up, kailangan mong tapusin ang required subjects at magpasa ng Certificate of Completion at Good Moral Character. Para sa SHS graduation, kailangan tapusin lahat ng subjects sa Grade 11 at 12!

patterns: kailangan ba mag submit ng projects
responses: 🐾 Performance tasks are part of the grading system and are required in every subject. Projects may vary depending on your teacher—always check with them for specific instructions and deadlines!
📝 Yes! Performance tasks are mandatory for all subjects as they're part of your grade. Projects depend on your teacher's requirements, so stay updated with their announcements.
✅ Performance tasks are required across all subjects. For specific projects, consult your subject teachers—they'll provide detailed instructions and deadlines.
🐾 Oo, required ang performance tasks sa lahat ng subjects kasi part sila ng grade mo. Para sa specific projects, check with your teacher para sa instructions!

patterns: do i have to do work immersion
pwede ba hindi mag work immersion
responses: 🐾 Yes, Work Immersion is a required component for graduation in the Senior High School (SHS) program. It's mandatory under the K to 12 curriculum.
💼 Work Immersion is absolutely required for all SHS students—it's a key part of the K to 12 program and helps prepare you for your chosen career path!
✅ Work Immersion is mandatory for SHS graduation. It's an essential part of the K to 12 curriculum that gives you real-world experience in your field.
💼 Oo, required ang Work Immersion para sa lahat ng SHS students. Mandatory siya under K to 12 curriculum at tutulong sa'yo para sa career mo!
🐾 You can view your grades during the scheduled card distribution day. Your teachers and advisers will be available to discuss your performance. We encourage you to attend with a parent or guardian!
📊 Grades are available during card distribution day! Your adviser and teachers will be there to explain your performance and answer questions. Bring your parent or guardian along!
📋 Check your grades on card distribution day when teachers and advisers are available. It's a great time to discuss your progress—come with a parent or guardian if possible!
📊 Makikita mo ang grades mo during card distribution day. Nandyan ang teachers at advisers para pag-usapan ang performance mo. Dalhin mo parent or guardian mo!
🐾 If you fail a subject, you'll need to retake it to meet academic requirements. Don't worry—your teachers are here to support you! With effort and commitment, you can improve and succeed.
💪 Failing a subject means you'll retake it, but you're not alone! Your teachers will help guide you through it. Stay committed and you'll get there!
📚 You'll need to retake any failed subject. Remember, your teachers support you every step of the way. With dedication, you can definitely improve!
💪 Pag bumagsak ka sa subject, kailangan mong i-retake. Pero huwag mag-alala, nandito ang teachers mo para tumulong! With effort, kaya mo yan!

🐾 Yes, you may transfer to a different strand in Senior High School. However, you'll need to take any subjects required in your new strand that you haven't completed yet. Consult with the academic coordinator or your adviser for guidance!
🔄 Transferring strands is possible! Just note that you'll need to complete any new required subjects you missed from your previous strand. Talk to your adviser or academic coordinator to plan it out.
✅ Strand transfers are allowed, but you must fulfill the new strand's required subjects. Your adviser or the academic coordinator can help you navigate the process smoothly!
🔄 Pwede mag-transfer ng strand! Pero kailangan mong tapusin ang required subjects ng new strand mo. Kausapin ang adviser or academic coordinator para sa guidance!

📝 To enroll, visit the Registrar's Office (Building A) with:
• PSA Birth Certificate
• Report Card (SF9)
• Good Moral Certificate
🚫 No entrance exam required!
✅ We accept transferees until the end of the 2nd quarter.

📋 Enrollment is easy! Head to the Registrar's Office in Building A with your PSA Birth Certificate, SF9 Report Card, and Good Moral Certificate. No entrance exam needed, and we welcome transferees until Q2 ends!

✅ Bring your PSA Birth Certificate, Report Card (SF9), and Good Moral Certificate to the Registrar's Office (Building A) to enroll. No entrance exam, and transferees are accepted until the 2nd quarter ends!

📝 Para mag-enroll, punta sa Registrar's Office (Building A) with PSA Birth Certificate, Report Card (SF9), at Good Moral Certificate. Walang entrance exam! Tumatanggap kami ng transferees until 2nd quarter!

patterns: what time should i be in school
responses:
📅 School year starts in June.
⏰ Class hours by grade:
• Grade 7: 6:00 AM – 12:30 PM
• Grade 8: 12:40 PM – 7:00 PM
• Grade 9: 1:00 PM – 7:20 PM
• Grade 10: 6:00 AM – 12:40 PM
• Grade 11: 6:00 AM – 12:30 PM
• Grade 12: 12:45 PM – 7:30 PM

⏰ The school year kicks off in June! Here are the required arrival times:
• Grade 7, 10 & 11 → 6:00 AM
• Grade 8 → 12:40 PM
• Grade 9 → 1:00 PM
• Grade 12 → 12:45 PM
We recommend arriving a few minutes early!
📅 Classes begin in June each year. Schedule varies: Grades 7, 10, 11 have morning classes starting at 6:00 AM, and Grades 8, 9, 12 have afternoon classes starting around 12:40–1:00 PM.
📅 Nagsisimula ang school year sa June. Iba-iba ang schedule per grade: Grade 7, 10, 11 ay umaga (6:00 AM start), Grade 8, 9, 12 ay hapon (12:40 PM–1:00 PM start).

patterns: may program ba para sa disabilities
responses: 💡 Yes! We have a Special Education (SPED) Program to support students with special needs or disabilities.
💙 Our SPED Program is here to help students with special needs or disabilities. We're committed to inclusive education!
✨ Absolutely! GTDLNHS offers a Special Education (SPED) Program for students with special needs, ensuring everyone gets quality education.
💙 Oo! May SPED Program kami para sa students with special needs or disabilities. Committed kami sa inclusive education!

• Guidance Office
• Library

🏢 Our campus has everything you need: Computer Lab, Science Lab, Library, School Clinic, Canteen, Multi-purpose Hall, Guidance Office, and more!
✨ We're equipped with modern facilities like Computer and Science Laboratories, a Library, School Clinic, Canteen, Multi-purpose Hall, and comfortable amenities for students!
🏫 May complete facilities kami: Computer Lab, Science Lab, Library, Clinic, Canteen, Multi-purpose Hall, Guidance Office, at marami pang iba!

🌟 Plenty of clubs and organizations to explore! We have Math, Science, Language, English, Computers, TLE, and special clubs like Ecosavers, Ang Daluyan, and The Genzette. Find your passion!
🎓 Join one of our 15+ clubs including academic clubs (Math, Science, English), special interest groups (Ecosavers, Nexus), and creative organizations like The Genzette!
🎯 Maraming clubs na pwede mong salihan: Math, Science, English, Computers, TLE, at special clubs like Ecosavers, Ang Daluyan, at The Genzette!
🐾 Absolutely! Most clubs open membership at the start of the school year, so keep an eye out for announcements.
✅ Yes! Clubs typically open for new members at the beginning of the school year. Watch for announcements from your teachers or the bulletin boards!
🎉 Of course! Club recruitment happens early in the school year. Stay tuned to school announcements so you don't miss out!
✅ Oo! Karamihan ng clubs ay nag-oopen ng membership sa start ng school year. Abangan mo ang announcements!
🐾 You may reach out to Sir Arnielson Calubiran or visit the SSLG headquarters to file your candidacy form.
🗳️ Interested in SSG? Contact Sir Arnielson Calubiran or drop by the SSLG headquarters to get your candidacy form and start your journey!
💼 To run for SSG, file your candidacy form at the SSLG headquarters or speak with Sir Arnielson Calubiran for guidance!
🗳️ Interested sa SSG? Kausapin si Sir Arnielson Calubiran or punta sa SSLG headquarters para sa candidacy form!
💙 Our Guidance Office is here for academic, personal, career, and social concerns. Drop by anytime for support.
🤗 Need someone to talk to? The Guidance Office welcomes you for academic, personal, career, or social support anytime!
💙 Whether it's academics, personal issues, or career guidance, our Guidance Office is always ready to help. Don't hesitate to visit!
💙 Nandito ang Guidance Office para sa academic, personal, career, at social concerns. Punta ka anytime para sa support!

👕 Junior High: Uniform Mon–Thu, PE attire Fri.
👕 Senior High: PE attire on HOPE day, uniforms on other days.
👔 JHS students wear uniforms Monday to Thursday and PE attire on Friday. SHS students wear PE attire on HOPE day and regular uniforms on other days.
🎽 Dress code: JHS wears school uniform Mon–Thu and PE attire on Friday. SHS wears PE attire only during HOPE classes, uniforms otherwise.
👕 JHS: Uniform Mon–Thu, PE attire Fri. SHS: PE attire sa HOPE day, uniform sa other days.

👨‍🏫 GTDLNHS has dedicated and passionate teachers across all subjects! For specific faculty information, please contact the Registrar's Office or visit the school's official Facebook page.

💰 GTDLNHS is a public school, so there are NO tuition fees! You may have minimal contributions for school projects or activities. For specific details, contact the Registrar's Office.
🏫 Great news! As a public school, we don't charge tuition fees. There may be small contributions for projects or materials, but education here is free!
✅ GTDLNHS is a public school—education is FREE! Any minimal fees are for school activities or projects. Contact the office for more details.
💰 Public school kami, so WALANG tuition fees! May minimal contributions lang para sa projects or activities. Education dito ay FREE!
📅 The school calendar includes important dates for enrollment, exams, holidays, and events. For the most updated calendar, check with your advisers or visit the school's official Facebook page!
🗓️ Key dates like exams, holidays, and school events are announced regularly. Stay updated through your adviser, bulletin boards, or our Facebook page!
📆 The academic calendar is available from your advisers and posted on school bulletin boards. For real-time updates, follow GTDLNHS on Facebook!
📅 Ang school calendar ay may important dates para sa enrollment, exams, holidays, at events. Para sa latest, check with advisers or Facebook page!
📋 Report cards (SF9) are distributed during scheduled card-giving days each quarter. Your adviser will announce the date and time. Bring a parent or guardian!
🎓 Card distribution happens at the end of each quarter. Your adviser will inform you of the schedule—make sure to attend with your parent or guardian!
📊 Report cards are released quarterly during card-giving days. Check with your adviser for the exact schedule and remember to bring your parent or guardian!
📋 Ang report cards (SF9) ay ibibigay during card-giving days each quarter. Announce ng adviser mo ang date. Dalhin mo parent or guardian mo!
📚 The library is open during school hours for students to study, read, and borrow books. For specific hours and borrowing policies, please ask the librarian or check with your adviser!
📖 Our library welcomes students during school hours! Visit the librarian for borrowing guidelines and available resources.
🏫 The library is accessible throughout the school day. Feel free to visit for studying or borrowing books—just follow the librarian's guidelines!
📚 Open ang library during school hours para mag-study, magbasa, at mag-borrow ng books. Para sa specific hours, tanungin ang librarian!
🍱 The school canteen offers affordable meals and snacks for students! Menus vary daily, so visit during break time to see what's available.
🥪 Our canteen provides budget-friendly meals and snacks throughout the day. Drop by during breaks to grab something delicious!
🍽️ The canteen serves various meals and snacks at affordable prices. Perfect for lunch or quick snacks between classes!
🍱 Nag-aalok ang canteen ng affordable meals at snacks! Iba-iba ang menu daily, so punta during break time para makita!
🏥 The school clinic is available during school hours for medical emergencies, first aid, and health concerns. Visit the clinic or ask for the school nurse if you need help!
💊 Our school clinic provides first aid and basic medical care during school hours. Don't hesitate to visit if you're not feeling well!
🩺 The clinic is open during school hours for health emergencies and medical needs. The school nurse is ready to assist you anytime!
🏥 Available ang clinic during school hours para sa medical emergencies, first aid, at health concerns. Punta sa clinic kung kailangan mo ng tulong!

🎓 As a public school, education is free! For additional financial assistance programs or scholarships, contact the Guidance Office or Registrar's Office for information on available government programs.
💙 GTDLNHS is a public school with free education! For info on government scholarships or assistance programs, visit the Guidance Office.
✨ Education here is free! If you need information about government scholarships or financial assistance, the Guidance Office can guide you!
🎓 Public school kami, so free ang education! Para sa additional financial assistance or scholarships, contact ang Guidance Office para sa info!
🏀 GTDLNHS has various sports programs and teams! For information about tryouts, practices, and joining varsity teams, contact the PE Department or check announcements from coaches.
⚽ We have active sports programs including basketball, volleyball, and more! Reach out to the PE teachers or watch for tryout announcements.
🏐 Sports and athletics are part of our vibrant school culture! Contact PE teachers or attend school assemblies for updates on teams and tryouts.
🏀 May various sports programs at teams kami! Para sa info about tryouts at practices, contact ang PE Department o check ang announcements!
💻 The Computer Laboratory is available for classes and educational activities. Usage outside class hours may be allowed with teacher supervision—check with the lab coordinator!
🖥️ Our Computer Lab is primarily for scheduled classes. For personal use, ask your ICT teacher or lab coordinator about availability and policies.
⌨️ The Computer Laboratory is equipped for technology classes! Outside class hours, check with the lab coordinator for access and guidelines.
💻 Available ang Computer Lab para sa classes at educational activities. Para sa usage outside class hours, check with lab coordinator!
🔬 The Science Laboratory is equipped for hands-on experiments in your Science classes! It's used during scheduled lab activities with teacher supervision.
⚗️ Our Science Lab provides the tools for exciting experiments! You'll use it during science classes under your teacher's guidance for safe, engaging learning.
🧪 The Science Laboratory supports your learning with real experiments! Access is during scheduled science classes with proper teacher supervision for safety.
🔬 Equipped ang Science Lab para sa hands-on experiments sa Science classes! Ginagamit during scheduled lab activities with teacher supervision.

🛡️ Bullying is NOT tolerated at GTDLNHS. If you're experiencing or witnessing bullying, please report it immediately to your adviser, the Guidance Office, or any teacher you trust. Your safety matters!
💙 We take bullying seriously. If you're being bullied or see someone being harassed, reach out to the Guidance Office, your adviser, or any trusted adult right away. You're not alone!
⚠️ Your safety is our priority! Report any bullying or harassment to the Guidance Office or your adviser immediately. We're here to protect and support you.
🛡️ Hindi tolerated ang bullying sa GTDLNHS. Kung binubully ka o may nakita kang bullying, i-report agad sa adviser, Guidance Office, o kahit anong teacher na pinagkakatiwalaan mo!
🔍 Lost something? Check with the Guidance Office or your adviser—they handle lost and found items. Found something? Please turn it in so we can return it to its owner!
📦 For lost and found items, visit the Guidance Office. They keep track of found belongings and can help you locate your missing items!
🎒 Lost an item? Contact the Guidance Office or your adviser. Found something? Please submit it to the office so the owner can claim it!
🔍 May nawala? Check with Guidance Office or adviser mo—sila ang nag-hahandle ng lost and found. May nahanap? I-submit sa office para maibalik sa owner!
🪪 For school ID concerns (new ID, validation, or replacement), visit the Registrar's Office in Building A. They'll assist you with the requirements and process!
📇 Need your school ID or have ID issues? Head to the Registrar's Office (Building A) for assistance with new IDs, validation, or replacements.
🆔 The Registrar's Office handles all ID-related concerns including issuance, validation, and replacement. Visit Building A for help!
🪪 Para sa school ID concerns (new ID, validation, o replacement), punta sa Registrar's Office sa Building A. Tutulungan ka nila!
👨‍👩‍👧 Parent-Teacher Conferences are scheduled periodically, usually during card-giving days. Your adviser will announce the schedule—parents are encouraged to attend to discuss your progress!
🤝 PTCs happen regularly, often during card distribution. Your adviser will inform you of dates—it's a great time for parents and teachers to connect about your academic journey!
📅 Parent-Teacher Conferences are held throughout the year, typically aligned with card-giving days. Watch for announcements from your adviser!
👨‍👩‍👧 Ang Parent-Teacher Conferences ay scheduled periodically, usually during card-giving days. Announce ng adviser mo ang schedule—encouraged ang parents na dumalo!

patterns: what should i do if i am absent
what happens when i miss class
responses: 📝 If you're absent, submit an excuse letter from your parent/guardian to your adviser when you return. For prolonged absences due to health, a medical certificate may be required.
🏥 Absences require an excuse letter from your parent or guardian. Submit it to your adviser upon your return. Medical certificates are needed for extended medical absences.
📋 When you're absent, bring an excuse letter signed by your parent/guardian to your adviser. For illness-related absences, you may need a medical certificate.
📝 Pag absent ka, mag-submit ng excuse letter from parent/guardian mo sa adviser when you return. Para sa prolonged absences, kailangan ng medical certificate.

⚠️ Leaving school before the official dismissal time is generally NOT allowed. This policy ensures your safety and maintains order. If you have an urgent reason, coordinate in advance with your class adviser or the school office. Leaving without clearance may result in disciplinary action.
🚫 Early departure is generally not permitted to keep students safe. If you absolutely need to leave early, please inform your class adviser or the school office beforehand and get proper clearance.
📋 The school discourages leaving before dismissal time. For urgent cases, coordinate with your adviser in advance. Leaving without proper clearance may lead to disciplinary action.
⚠️ Hindi karaniwang pinapayagan ang pag-uwi nang maaga. Kung mayroon kang urgent na dahilan, makipag-coordinate muna sa iyong adviser o sa school office bago umalis!
